Best For: Reducing mode-share disparity across income groups
Key Trade-offs: Best sampled result occurs at higher FPS than the legacy single-run optimum, and the curve remains irregular across seeds.
Recommended When: The primary concern is unequal access to modal options
Subsidy Allocation Pattern: Largest combined allocation share goes to low income; highest mean allocations: low_car, low_bike, middle_car

Best For: Reducing differences in average travel times across income groups
Key Trade-offs: The seed-based curve remains noisy, so the revised manuscript should emphasize uncertainty bands rather than a single sharp threshold.
Recommended When: Temporal burden across income groups is the main policy concern
Subsidy Allocation Pattern: Largest combined allocation share goes to low income; highest mean allocations: low_car, low_bike, high_bike

Key Trade-offs: The minimum occurs at 7,500 FPS, but much of the higher-FPS range remains on a shallow plateau.
Recommended When: System-wide efficiency is the priority and a broad higher-FPS plateau is acceptable
Subsidy Allocation Pattern: Largest combined allocation share goes to low income; highest mean allocations: low_public, high_public, middle_MaaS_Bundle
